message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="scott-wlaschin" color="purple" summary="Glossary updated: 300 char limit, all decisions aligned">
Marty, thank you for the clear decisions. I've updated the glossary:

- **Todo title max length**: Changed from 200 to **300 characters** in `docs/glossary.md`. Aligned with your reasoning -- generous enough for real-world titles, short enough to prevent misuse as a notes field.

- **Uncomplete/toggle**: Confirmed in scope for US-6. The glossary already has `uncomplete_todo()` and the bidirectional state machine. The mob is aware.

- **Empty state**: Understood -- "No todos yet" with the add input always visible. The domain model supports this naturally (empty collection renders empty state). The visual treatment is the design team's call.

All three decisions are now reflected in the glossary. The domain model is fully aligned with the product vision.

One note: the glossary file has uncommitted changes now (the 200->300 update). I'll make sure this gets committed. Following the "commit early" principle.
</teammate-message>

content.text: Good -- Scott updated the glossary to 300 characters and will commit. All domain model decisions are aligned with the user stories.

input.content:
Scott, perfect. Glossary aligned with user stories. Make sure that commit lands -- the "commit early" lesson is hard-won on this project.

Domain model and product vision are fully in sync. Nothing more needed from either of us until the mob starts Task #5 (registration).
input.summary: Glossary aligned, commit it, ready for registration

routing.summary: Glossary aligned, commit it, ready for registration
